high_priority_proposals.design: 2x2: condition A = committed BG gate active, condition B = gate ablated (beta suppressed), crossed with stable vs volatile environment. 2 matched seeds. PASS: harm_committed < harm_ablated in stable env; gap narrows or reverses in volatile env.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Depends on ARC-016 (provisional) and MECH-090 (active). This is the behavioral consequence layer split from ARC-016 into ARC-029 during 2026-03-22 governance session.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Test whether committed operating mode produces measurably lower harm outcomes than ablated (uncommitted) mode, and whether this gap narrows in volatile environments (gate-ablation design).

key_sources: Balleine & O'Doherty (2010) - Human and rodent homologues in action control: corticostriatal determinants of goal-directed and habitual action
DLS/DMS lesion studies distinguishing outcome-sensitive vs stimulus-response behavior
Beta oscillation persistence during committed action sequences (Khanna & Carmena 2015 or similar)
Daw et al. (2005) - Uncertainty-based competition between prefrontal and dorsolateral striatal systems for behavioral control
high_priority_proposals.objective: Ground ARC-029 in habitual vs goal-directed neuroscience: dorsolateral vs dorsomedial striatum dissociation, beta persistence during habitual sequences, model-free/model-based dual-process accounts.

key_sources: Gallese et al. (1996) / Rizzolatti & Craighero (2004) - Mirror neuron system and action understanding
Kording & Wolpert (2004) - Bayesian integration in sensorimotor learning (causal inference framework)
Decety & Lamm (2007) - The role of the right temporoparietal junction in social interaction
Wolpert, Ghahramani & Jordan (1995) - An internal model for sensorimotor integration (efference copy)
high_priority_proposals.objective: Ground MECH-072 (foreseeable-harm gating reduces false attribution) in causal attribution neuroscience: mirror neuron agency detection, Bayesian causal inference, TPJ role in intentional attribution, cerebellar efference copy in self/other discrimination.

high_priority_proposals.design: Condition A = harm-surprise-gated beta variance (unexpected harm spike triggers variance increase). Condition B = jitter-only control (no harm-triggered spike). PASS: A shows significantly higher beta_variance on unexpected harm events than B; B shows stable low-level jitter only.
high_priority_proposals.notes: EXQ-049e PASS validated Route-1 (noise floor preventing variance collapse). This experiment validates Route-2 (harm-surprise triggering volatility spike). Both routes needed for full MECH-104 validation.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Validate Route-2 MECH-104 wiring: unexpected harm events should spike commitment uncertainty (beta variance) via surprise gate, distinct from the jitter noise floor baseline.

high_priority_proposals.executed_note: All four key sources now grounded in targeted_review_connectome_mech_104: Yu & Dayan 2005 (yudayan2005), Aston-Jones & Cohen 2005 (astonjones2005), Bouret & Sara 2005 (bouret2005), Sara 2009 (sara2009, added this session); Sara & Bouret 2012 orienting/reorienting (sarabouret2012) supplements as bonus source.
key_sources: Yu & Dayan (2005) - Uncertainty, neuromodulation and attention (expected vs unexpected uncertainty)
Aston-Jones & Cohen (2005) - An integrative theory of locus coeruleus-norepinephrine function: adaptive gain and optimal performance
Sara (2009) - The locus coeruleus and noradrenergic modulation of cognition
Bouret & Sara (2005) - Network reset: a simplified overarching theory of locus coeruleus noradrenaline function
high_priority_proposals.objective: Deepen biological grounding of MECH-104 (LC-NE volatility interrupt): Yu & Dayan 2005 expected/unexpected uncertainty distinction, Aston-Jones & Cohen adaptive gain theory, Sara norepinephrine and memory consolidation, Bouret & Sara network reset hypothesis.

high_priority_proposals.design: Condition A: current post-SD-011 attribution pathway on z_harm_s (same architectural path as the successful 166e-style result). Condition B: explicit ablation or scramble control that preserves training budget but destroys the attribution mechanism. Use at least 3 matched seeds and one simple task variant. PASS: trained condition beats control on pre-registered attribution gap / harm-delta discrimination metrics with consistent sign across seeds and preserves escalation ordering. This is the first-paper attribution anchor and should be narratively simple.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Run first. This is the clean paper-1 anchor for responsibility-like attribution in the current V3 substrate.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Reconfirm the post-SD-011 SD-003 attribution result in its current form: can the trained z_harm_s counterfactual attribution path cleanly beat an explicit ablation or scramble control across matched seeds?

high_priority_proposals.design: Condition A: current dual-stream SD-011 stack (z_harm_s + z_harm_a active). Condition B: fused or stream-ablated control that collapses the distinction. Use matched seeds and one task variant that changes hazard structure without changing the core story. PASS: the dual-stream condition preserves the expected sensory-vs-affective dissociation and beats control on the pre-registered substrate metrics in the same direction across seeds and variant. This is a stability/replication pack, not a new theory search.
high_priority_proposals.notes: This is the minimum substrate-stability check needed before treating Claim 1 as platform instead of ongoing rescue work. Platform-stability diagnostic pilot queued as V3-EXQ-472 2026-04-21; main evidence script authored after pilot recovery_ratio observed.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Test whether the dual nociceptive stream substrate is stable enough to be treated as platform rather than rescue work.

high_priority_proposals.design: Condition A: benefit path receives a handcrafted or oracle-grade resource indicator / goal cue that is invariant to spatial respawn. Condition B: matched sham or zero-goal control. Keep the rest of the stack unchanged. PASS: Condition A yields a clear navigation / resource-acquisition lift over control without requiring a new learned encoder. Interpretation: PASS -> selection path is usable and learned representation is the main remaining gap. FAIL -> action selection / benefit integration is still broken even with a good signal.
high_priority_proposals.notes: This diagnostic should be run before more elaborate goal-encoder work if the current goal stack remains ambiguous.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Resolve the representation-vs-selection ambiguity by injecting a handcrafted resource indicator into the goal path: if the selector gets an explicit goal cue, can it exploit it behaviorally?

high_priority_proposals.design: Condition A: GOAL_PRESENT with the current best learned goal pathway active. Condition B: GOAL_ABSENT or goal-ablated matched control. Use matched seeds and pre-register a behavioral lift criterion based on resource acquisition / navigation success, while also checking that harm does not regress catastrophically. PASS: GOAL_PRESENT produces a reproducible positive behavioral lift over control. If this fails while EXP-0091 passes, the learned representation remains the bottleneck; if both fail, the selector/integration path remains the bottleneck.
high_priority_proposals.notes: This is the decisive behavioral test for whether positive attractors are real in the current V3 stack.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Show genuine goal-directed behavior with the current best learned goal signal: does GOAL_PRESENT beat GOAL_ABSENT under matched conditions?

high_priority_proposals.design: Condition A: COMBINED selector with harm and goal evaluation active on the same candidate trajectories. Condition B: HARM_ONLY selector with goal pressure removed but identical trajectory generation and evaluation budget. Use a goal-bearing environment with non-trivial hazard pressure. PASS: COMBINED improves goal achievement or resource acquisition over HARM_ONLY without paying an unacceptable harm penalty. This is the cleanest behavioral test that approach and avoidance are adjudicated in one selector rather than bolted together externally.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Paper-1 selector payoff test. If needed later, compare COMBINED vs GOAL_ONLY as a follow-up rather than broadening this first pair.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Demonstrate the selector payoff directly: does a shared harm-plus-goal selector outperform a one-sided selector in a hazard-bearing goal task?

high_priority_proposals.design: 3x3 grid sweep of hazard_rate (low/medium/high) x resource_rate (low/medium/high). For each cell, 3 seeds in HABIT and PLANNED mode. Measure HABIT/PLANNED behavioral equivalence score (|HABIT_harm_rate - PLANNED_harm_rate|). PASS: identify threshold ratio above which gap < 0.02 in majority (>=2/3) of seeds; produce ratio-to-gap curve showing transition.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Immediately testable -- no new architecture needed, parameter extension of EXQ-237a. Key deliverable is the threshold estimate, not just PASS/FAIL.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Characterize the hazard/resource ratio threshold above which the depression attractor forms (behavioral flattening: HABIT==PLANNED). EXQ-237a established the attractor exists at LONG_HORIZON parameters; this sweep identifies boundary conditions.

high_priority_proposals.design: Multi-seed (5 seeds) replication of LONG_HORIZON condition from EXQ-237a (high hazard/resource ratio). Measure per seed: z_harm_a_mean, z_goal_norm, HABIT_harm_rate, PLANNED_harm_rate, behavioral_equivalence_score. PASS: >=4/5 seeds show z_goal_norm < 0.1 AND |HABIT_harm_rate - PLANNED_harm_rate| < 0.02 (modes indistinguishable).
high_priority_proposals.notes: EXQ-237a seed=42 is preliminary evidence. This experiment provides the systematic replication needed to register INV-053 as active. Run before MECH-186/187/188 experiments.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Systematically replicate and characterize the computational depression attractor from EXQ-237a seed=42. Establish that z_harm_a elevation + z_goal failure -> HABIT/PLANNED flattening is robust across seeds.

high_priority_proposals.design: Phase 1: train 3 seeds in LONG_HORIZON until depression attractor established (z_goal_norm < 0.1 for 50+ episodes). Phase 2: switch to LOW_HARM environment, continue training 300 additional episodes. Measure recovery trajectory: z_goal_norm and HABIT/PLANNED gap over phase 2 episodes. PASS: recovery trajectory shows latency >50 phase-2 episodes before z_goal_norm crosses 0.3 threshold (demonstrating delayed/phase-transition recovery, not immediate). FAIL: z_goal_norm recovers within 10 episodes (graded not phase-transition).
high_priority_proposals.notes: INV-054 maintenance loop predicts that the depressive state is self-maintaining: z_goal absent -> no terrain exploration -> terrain stays collapsed. Recovery requires accumulated terrain re-exposure to cross a threshold. This is a unique prediction distinguishing INV-054 from simple reward-signal insufficiency accounts. V3-EXQ-435 supersedes V3-EXQ-278 with sustained-crossing recovery criterion (10 consecutive eps above 0.3, avg over last 30 steps) to distinguish attractor escape from single-episode bounces.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Test the phase-transition recovery prediction: once the depression attractor is established (LONG_HORIZON), switching to LOW_HARM environment produces non-linear delayed recovery, not immediate improvement.

high_priority_proposals.design: 5-condition x 3-seed: (A) SIMPLE/no_interventions (healthy baseline), (B) LONG_HORIZON/no_interventions (depression baseline), (C) LONG_HORIZON/terrain_floor_only (MECH-186), (D) LONG_HORIZON/elevated_gain_only (MECH-187), (E) LONG_HORIZON/goal_injection_only (MECH-188). Compare z_goal_norm and HABIT/PLANNED gap for each condition vs healthy baseline. PASS: no single mechanism (C, D, or E) recovers to >= 80% of SIMPLE baseline on both metrics; partial rescue by each individually confirms distinct stages but joint necessity.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Depends on EXP-0098, EXP-0099, EXP-0100 completing first for parameter calibration of each mechanism. Should run after those three complete. A full combined-mechanisms condition (all three together) can optionally be added as condition F to test whether all three together do restore baseline.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Test whether any single serotonergic rescue mechanism (MECH-186, 187, or 188 in isolation) fully restores LONG_HORIZON performance to SIMPLE baseline, or whether full recovery requires all three. Confirms INV-052: tonic regulatory system is necessary across all three pipeline stages.

key_sources: Huys, Daw & Dayan (2021) - Depression: A decision-theoretic analysis (Annual Review of Neuroscience) -- reward learning in depression, pruning, and effort
Adams, Huys & Roiser (2016) - Computational psychiatry: towards a mathematically informed understanding of mental illness (Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery & Psychiatry)
Dayan & Huys (2008) - Serotonin, inhibition, and negative mood (PLOS Computational Biology) -- 5-HT and reward modulation
Eshel & Roiser (2010) - Reward and punishment processing in depression (Biological Psychiatry) -- anhedonia as reward sensitivity deficit
Treadway & Zald (2011) - Reconsidering anhedonia in depression: lessons from translational neuroscience (Neuroscience and Biobehavioral Reviews) -- wanting vs liking in clinical anhedonia
high_priority_proposals.notes: Key framing contribution: REE locates depression at the terrain/seeding layer, not the value-learning layer. This is architecturally distinct from existing computational psychiatry models and should be documented as such in the paper. Executed 2026-04-18: 4 of 5 proposed sources already covered in targeted_review_inv_053 (Adams 2016, Dayan 2008, Eshel 2010, Treadway 2010); added Huys/Daw/Dayan 2015 decision-theoretic review as 5th entry (the proposal's '2021' date was incorrect -- the Annual Review paper is 2015, PMID 25705929).
high_priority_proposals.objective: Ground INV-053 (computational depression attractor) in computational psychiatry, distinguishing motivational terrain collapse from aberrant reward learning. Huys/Dayan/Adams lineage focuses on incorrect Q-values and precision weighting; INV-053 is about the substrate (benefit terrain, z_goal seeding) degrading. Establish this as a distinct failure mode.

high_priority_proposals.design: Gate: EXQ-247 must PASS first (z_goal_norm >= 0.1). Then re-queue EXQ-228a protocol (THETA_ACTIVE vs THETA_ZEROED, 3 seeds, resource_rate lift >= 0.05). No redesign needed.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Design-forward analysis DR-1: z_goal seeding requires SD-012 resolution. See governance_meta_illusory_conflict_resolution.md and design_forward_analysis_2026-04-06.md.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Re-run EXQ-228a theta bypass behavioral test after EXQ-247 resolves z_goal seeding. Protocol unchanged -- the experiment design is sound, only the substrate precondition (z_goal_norm >= 0.05) was unmet.

high_priority_proposals.design: Gate 1: EXQ-247 PASS. Gate 2: z_goal alignment test (re-run EXQ-235 protocol). If both pass, new Go/NoGo experiment with approach sub-channel activated by real z_goal. 2x2: Go_active vs Go_ablated x NoGo_active vs NoGo_ablated.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Design-forward analysis DR-2: z_goal norm and alignment are independent failure modes. SD-015 (resource indicator encoding) is a separate prerequisite.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Test Go/NoGo sub-channel symmetry with real z_goal. Requires both z_goal_norm >= 0.1 AND z_goal alignment with resource proximity (align_corr > 0.2). Two gates.
high_priority_proposals.supersedes: EXQ-235 (non-contributory: z_goal alignment failure), EXQ-086 (measurement bug)

high_priority_proposals.design: Add context-conditioned harm_eval threshold: harm_threshold = f(active_context_slot). In DANGEROUS context, lower threshold (more cautious). In SAFE context, higher threshold (more exploratory). Re-run EXQ-242 conditions (WAKING_ONLY, SWS_ONLY, SWS_THEN_REM). PASS: C1 (slot diff) AND C2 (harm rate benefit from context-conditioning). 5 seeds.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Design-forward analysis DR-6: missing link is between context_memory readout and action selection. The simplest proxy is context-conditioned harm_eval threshold.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Redesigned sleep phase ablation with context-conditioned action selection. EXQ-242 showed slot differentiation works (4/5 seeds) but agent doesn't use context knowledge. Add context->action pathway.
high_priority_proposals.supersedes: EXQ-242 (diagnostic: slot differentiation works but no behavioral path)

high_priority_proposals.design: Train E2_harm_s with SD-013 interventional contrast (counterfactual perturbation during training). Evaluate residual_harm = z_harm_s_observed - E2_harm_s(z_harm_s_{t-1}, a_actual) per step. Tag steps by event type (approach-to-harm, self-caused-hazard, externally-caused-hazard, baseline). Compute residual magnitude per event class, expect partial attenuation on self-caused relative to externally-caused (Shergill ~50% pattern). PASS: C1 forward_r2 >= 0.9; C2 self-vs-external attenuation ratio between 0.3-0.7 (partial, not binary); C3 SNR > 3 on approach-to-harm events. Event-density control: reject seeds with <20 agent-caused-hazard events; resample until density target met. 4 seeds.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Successor-to-SD-003 test. Single-pass comparator, no counterfactual branch. Script to be written as v3_exq_433_sd029_eventcond_comparator.py; not yet queued -- pending script implementation.
high_priority_proposals.objective: First V3 test of SD-029 (single-pass z_harm_s comparator). Replace mean-cf-gap with event-conditioned residual analysis on approach-to-harm events; include event-density controls (guaranteed minimum agent-caused-hazard events per seed) to fix the EXQ-431 sample-starvation failure mode.
